Daniel Nnodim

Computer Science × Economics

I build at the intersection of finance, software, and research. My work combines full-stack development, financial analysis, data-driven thinking, and a practical interest in how technology can make better decisions easier.

Selected Work

Projects I have built and explored.

TeamBlueLock

A financial operations platform for small businesses. The app brings inventory, ingredient costing, recipe management, and profit analysis into one dashboard.

Live Demo ↗
Next.js • MongoDB • Playwright • Jest

AI Stock Prediction Research

A research project where I tested machine-learning models on financial indicators and market data to understand how difficult short-term stock prediction can be in practice.

Python • Finance • Machine Learning

Personal Finance Tracker

A budgeting and financial management app with transaction tracking, spending categories, balance summaries, savings goals, and a demo dashboard for quick review.

View Demo Dashboard ↗
Flask • SQLite • Python • Finance

Roommate Matching Platform

A student housing concept focused on roommate discovery, profiles, messaging, and matching across schools.

React • Full Stack • Product Design

Multimedia Utility App

A multi-tool web app that brings everyday utilities into one place, including weather, calculations, currency conversion, QR scanning, notes, and a playable game.

Live Demo ↗
Next.js • React • Utility Tools

Financial Data Research Toolkit

A quantitative workflow using regression models, robust standard errors, multicollinearity checks, and financial datasets to support empirical analysis.

R • Econometrics • Quantitative Finance

Research

Research Question

Does R&D investment intensity predict annual stock returns differently across technology and non-technology firms?

Analysing the Impact of R&D on Stock Returns

Empirical finance study testing whether R&D investment intensity predicts annual stock returns for 100 publicly traded firms, split between technology and non-technology sectors.

OLS HC3 SE VIF Ramsey RESET

Model Logic

Short research design from inputs to interpretation.

01

Inputs

R&D intensity, firm size, profitability, and sector classification.

02

OLS Regression

Tested whether R&D investment predicts annual stock returns.

03

Finding

R² ≈ 0.01

No statistically significant R&D premium detected.

04

Interpretation

Consistent with Efficient Market Hypothesis reasoning.

About
